title: Removing Consecutive Repeated Characters from a String with Edge Cases (Case Sensitive)

statement.markdown:
Given a string of characters, implement a function to remove consecutive repeated characters while preserving the relative order of characters.
      This function should handle edge cases such as null input, empty string, and strings with repeating and non-repeating characters. Assume that the input is a string of single character strings separated by a single space (like "a b c").
statement.inputFormat: Single line string of space separated characters
statement.outputFormat: Modified string with consecutive repeated characters removed
statement.notes: Do not consider null or empty strings as valid inputs

examples.input: a a a b c c c b b

hints.text: Handle null and empty string inputs first.

editorial.title: Editorial Solution
editorial.intuition: Use a two-pointer approach where the first pointer traverses the string to identify repeated characters and the second pointer maintains the position for the next unique character.
editorial.bruteForce: Use a string concatenation to remove consecutive repeated characters which results in time complexity of O(n^2) due to repeated string concatenation.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a two-pointer approach to achieve a time complexity of O(n)
editorial.optimalSolution: Implement the optimal solution using two pointers.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Iterate over the string and prove that the two-pointer solution correctly removes consecutive repeated characters.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step execution of the two-pointer solution.
commonMistakes: Not handling null or empty string inputs

testCases.input:
3
a a a b c c c b b
